output: Implemented the rework for the context-audit completeness and fat-context guard coverage. The audit document now accounts for the current declaration set, including the split Lightbox/ImageGenerationForm surfaces and the additional test-only `React.createContext(...)` mock declarations. The guard now resolves React `createContext` calls through TypeScript symbols so both imported identifiers and namespaced/default-import forms are covered. Verification passed with the repro script, `npm run check:fat-contexts`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, a clean production grep for the removed panes API, and a full green `npm test` run.

`docs/state_management_context_audit.md` is matched by `.gitignore` (`*.md`), so the required audit-doc edit exists on disk but does not appear in `git status`.

task_updates.executor_notes: No new inventory scope changes were needed. The rework reused the existing repo-wide context and hook inventory, then revalidated the final declaration list against the updated audit document.
The panes store lifecycle contract and selector-first API were unchanged by this pass. No regression was introduced while fixing the audit and guard follow-up items.
No panes consumer or provider-tree changes were needed. Production panes access remains selector-based and the bootstrap slot remains in the established provider position.
The legacy panes API stayed removed from production usage throughout this pass. Verification later in T8 reconfirmed there are no production `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, or `usePanes()` imports.

Updated the audit document to reflect the current declaration-by-declaration inventory instead of the older umbrella summaries. The document now records 53 total declarations, including the split Lightbox/ImageGenerationForm contexts and four test-only declarations (`TabsContext`, `SegmentContext`, and the two existing `SelectContext` mocks), so the appendix and grouped tables reconcile to the same current snapshot.
No additional non-pane migrations were required on this pass. The previously narrowed over-threshold non-pane surfaces remain the audited end state.

Hardened the fat-context guard so identifier callees are resolved through TypeScript import symbols rather than literal identifier text. That keeps named-import aliases and `React.createContext(...)` namespace/default-import forms covered under the same counting rule documented in the audit.

Ran and deleted a short repro script to confirm the two review failures were addressed, then reran the required verification suite. `npm run check:fat-contexts`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, the production grep for removed panes APIs, and the full `npm test` suite all passed after the fixes.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: The original broad inventory remained sufficient for this rework. The updated audit doc now reconciles against the current declaration snapshot instead of older umbrella summaries.
No panes-store changes were required; the existing bootstrap/reset lifecycle contract remains intact.
No panes consumer migration changes were needed on this pass, and provider-tree mount order was left untouched.
The legacy panes API remained deleted from production usage; later grep verification still returned no production matches.

Confirmed by updating the document to a declaration-by-declaration 53-entry inventory with matching grouped tables and descriptor snapshot entries, including the split Lightbox/ImageGenerationForm declarations and the additional test-only mock contexts.
No over-threshold non-pane migration regressed on this pass; the previously narrowed/split surfaces remain the audited state.

Confirmed by updating the checker to resolve React `createContext` calls through TypeScript symbols for imported identifiers and namespace/default-import receivers, then rerunning `npm run check:fat-contexts` successfully.

Confirmed by running a throwaway repro script and deleting it afterward, then passing `npm run check:fat-contexts`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, the production grep for removed panes APIs, and the full `npm test` suite.
